capacity_matrix.readout_rule: locked online readout from 7.7b/7.7f; no retune
locked online readout from 7.7b/7.7f; no retune
locked online readout from 7.7b/7.7f; no retune
locked online readout from 7.7b/7.7f; no retune

diagnostic_questions.evidence_required: capacity curve over 16/32/64/128 units, paired seeds, tail MSE, and ESN-gap closure
diagnostic_questions.question: Does increasing CRA temporal-state capacity materially close the Lorenz gap toward matched-capacity ESN/reservoir references?

diagnostic_questions.evidence_required: capacity curve, repaired-stream manifest, lag/depth controls, and tail stability
diagnostic_questions.question: Does increasing CRA temporal-state capacity improve repaired NARMA10 memory-depth performance beyond v2.5-16?

diagnostic_questions.evidence_required: flat CRA curve despite matched-capacity sweep and separated shams
diagnostic_questions.question: If capacity increases do not help, does the result justify a structural mechanism rather than more units?

diagnostic_questions.evidence_required: matched-capacity baseline table with disclosed units and feature counts
diagnostic_questions.question: Do ESN/reservoir baselines remain stronger at comparable state counts, or is the comparison unfairly capacity-skewed?

diagnostic_questions.evidence_required: Mackey ratios and shams across capacity levels
diagnostic_questions.question: Does any higher-capacity variant preserve the Mackey signal, or does capacity damage the only confirmed standardized gain?

expected_artifacts.purpose: capacity diagnostic scoring result and classification
capacity scaling curves and gap closure

hypothesis: If the 7.7f failure is mainly capacity/state-interface limited, increasing CRA temporal-state capacity toward ESN parity should materially improve Lorenz and/or repaired NARMA while preserving the Mackey anchor and separating shams.
leakage_rules: use repaired NARMA U(0,0.2) stream from Tier 7.7e only
same seeds/tasks/splits across candidate, shams, and baselines
disclose feature counts and hidden units for every capacity variant
do not tune readout learning rates inside this gate
do not add mechanisms inside this gate
prediction emitted before online update for online models
normalization fit on train prefix only
mechanism_under_test: capacity/state-interface diagnostic only; no new CRA mechanism, no baseline freeze, no hardware/native transfer
next_gate: Tier 7.7h - Lorenz Capacity / NARMA Memory-Depth Scoring Gate
nonclaims: not evidence of external-baseline superiority unless the scoring gate shows it
not language, broad reasoning, AGI, or ASI evidence
null_hypothesis: Increasing CRA temporal-state capacity does not materially improve Lorenz or repaired NARMA, or apparent gains are explained by shams/overfit rather than usable state geometry or memory depth.

pass_fail_criteria.claim_allowed: capacity/state-interface limitation is supported; next step may tune capacity or design larger state interface before adding mechanisms
pass_fail_criteria.rule: Higher-capacity CRA improves Lorenz or repaired NARMA by >=25% versus 16-unit v2.5 and closes >=30% of the gap to matched-capacity ESN/reservoir with shams separated.

pass_fail_criteria.claim_allowed: capacity matters, but broad usefulness remains blocked; design targeted state-interface repair or mechanism only after this evidence
pass_fail_criteria.rule: Higher-capacity CRA improves materially versus 16-unit v2.5 but ESN/reservoir remains substantially better.

pass_fail_criteria.claim_allowed: more capacity alone is unlikely to solve Lorenz/NARMA; move to a predeclared structural mechanism gate
pass_fail_criteria.rule: CRA remains flat across 16/32/64/128 while matched-capacity ESN/reservoir remains much stronger.

pass_fail_criteria.claim_allowed: no capacity or mechanism promotion; repair leakage/overfit/task design first
pass_fail_criteria.rule: Train metrics improve while test/tail fails, or shams match candidate performance.

pass_fail_criteria.claim_allowed: do not promote larger capacity; preserve v2.5 localized claim only
pass_fail_criteria.rule: Capacity variants damage the confirmed Mackey signal without improving Lorenz/NARMA.

question: Are the remaining Lorenz and repaired-NARMA gaps capacity/state-interface limited, or do they remain flat under matched-capacity sweeps?

shams.purpose: reset temporal state periodically to verify state persistence matters
preserve input projection but scramble recurrent wiring
record feature counts so capacity claims are not hidden parameter-budget claims

tasks.primary_metric: test_mse and ESN-gap closure by capacity
tasks.why: 7.7f showed Lorenz is flat/weak while ESN dominates.

tasks.primary_metric: test_mse, tail_mse, and memory-depth improvement by capacity
tasks.why: 7.7f repaired finite streams but NARMA stayed near-flat.

tasks.primary_metric: ensure Mackey signal does not collapse under capacity sweep
tasks.why: Mackey is the localized positive signal from 7.7b/7.7f.
